Erica villosa, also known as Heather, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, potentially benefiting digestive health and immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Erica villosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Erica villosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaf extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
